sanskrit:
परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान् ।
पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम् ॥
transliteration:
paraṃ brahma paraṃ dhāma pavitraṃ paramaṃ bhavān |
puruṣaṃ śāśvataṃ divyam ādidevam ajaṃ vibhum ||
literal_meaning: You are the Supreme Brahman, the supreme abode, the purest, the ultimate; the eternal, divine Person, the primal God, unborn and all-pervading.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna moves from doubt to full recognition of Kṛṣṇa as the ground of being, integrating fragmented self-images into a single coherent identity anchored in the sacred.
psychological_state: Post-insight consolidation: cognitive coherence, affective stabilization, and secure attachment to an internalized benevolent archetype.
trigger_scenario: After therapy breakthrough, client suddenly sees parent not as oppressive but as wounded protector—same person, transformed perception.
anchor_text: Rest in the unchanging Self that holds all thoughts and worlds.
